title: centaurea virgata subsp. squarrosa
intro: Centaurea virgata subsp. squarrosa, also known as rough knapweed, is a plant species in the family Asteraceae. The plant has been used in traditional medicine due to its antimicrobial, anti-inflammatory, and antioxidant properties. The health benefits of Centaurea virgata subsp. squarrosa include its ability to aid in wound healing, reduce fever, and provide relief from respiratory issues. Herbal preparations of this plant can be found in the form of infusions, decoctions, and tinctures, although more research is needed to fully understand its potential uses and benefits.
